PlCounterfeitReports.Description: ALERT.Customers Beware, Counterfeit NIC Components products have been recently reported in the market.

PCNDto.DescriptionOfChange: Adjustment of barcode product labels and product marking
Adjustment of barcode product labels and product marking
Unification of TDK standard and Customer specific labeling

The introduction of laser marking on all production lines for boxed capacitors in Nashik, India, and Zhuhai, China, will shortly be completed. The remaining ink-jet marking will be phased out by the end of 2016. The laser marking may be at the top and/or on the side of the boxed film capacitors

Improvement of the ammo packs for film capacitors by changing the stick material from wood to plastic. These sticks are pushed through the holes of the taping to stabilize the shipment during transportation and avoid shifting of the capacitors.
This is to notify you of a Change in labeling, from the current 1D bar code to 2D Bar Code labeling.
An in-house film metallization and cutting process is already in place at several EPCOS plants. This process is now also being established at the plants in M¿laga, Spain, and Bawal, India.

An in-house film metallization and cutting process is already in place at several EPCOS plants. This process is now also being established at the plants in Málaga, Spain, and Bawal, India.These plants will then use in-house metallized film in addition to the metallized film supplied by qualified vendors for the manufacture of film capacitors
Alert, Fire destroys warehouse at EPCOS capacitor plant in Gravatai, Brazil

The laser marking process at the plant in Nashik, India, will be extended to all boxed and powder-dipped film capacitors of the EPCOS MKT and MKP type. Laser technology is resistant to cleaning agents and creates less solid waste than ink-jet marking.

Improvement of the packaging for capacitors in LS 27.5 with longer leads (> 6.0 mm) and box sizes of 16.0 x 32.0 x 31.5 mm^3 (w x h x l) and larger in order to protect the longer leads during the transportation.

EPCOS is introducing laser marking on all production lines for boxed and powder-dipped MKP film capacitors in its plant at Nashik, India. The new marking will be applied to the top or side of boxed and powder-dipped capacitors. The new laser marking offers some advantages compared to ink-jet marking, since it is resistant to most cleaning agents and creates less solid waste.
Transfer of the remaining production of LS 27.5 boxed film capacitors from Málaga, Spain, to the plants in Nashik, India, and Zhuhai, China.
Transfer of the remaining production of LS 27.5 boxed film capacitors from Málaga, Spain, to the plants in Nashik, India, and Zhuhai, China.
TDK-EPC is introducing a laser marking system for all EPCOS boxed MKP and MKT film capacitors at its plants in Gravataf, Brazil, as well as Zhuhai and Ningguo City, China.
Avoid the usage of dibutyl phthalate [CAS # 84-74-2; EC # 201-557-4] in the resin of boxed film capacitors
EPCOS is introducing laser marking equipment for all production lines in Malaga/ Spain for boxed capacitors.
EPCOS has been acquired by TDK.
